scenario.title: Third ADH Claim Filed with Unclear Prior Event Qualification
scenario.description: Customer files a third accidental damage from handling (ADH) claim within the Plan Term after two prior ADH-related service events, but one of the previous two events was for a screen malfunction initially classified as a hardware defect and subsequently repaired under Section 3.1 after investigation. The current damage is caused by a drop resulting in screen and internal component failure, occurring shortly after the second ADH claim was serviced.

service_fee.notes: Service fee does not apply since coverage is no longer provided after two ADH Service Events.

policy_references.snippet: ADH coverage will expire and all of Apple's obligations to you under this section 3.2 will be fulfilled before the end of the Plan Term when Apple, as a result of ADH claims, has provided to you two (2) Service Events.
You will pay a $49 (U.S.) dollar service fee plus applicable tax for each ADH claim.
If you cancel this Plan within thirty (30) days of your Plan receipt, you will receive a full refund less the value of any service provided under the Plan.

scenario.reasoning: The policy explicitly limits ADH coverage to two Service Events within the Plan Term (Section 3.2). Since the customer already exhausted these two ADH claims, this third damage incident falls outside the plan's coverage and thus does not require payment of the $49 service fee. Although the mechanical failure qualifies as an accidental damage event, the policy's numeric limit creates a coverage denial. However, the customer may still be eligible for hardware service claims under Section 3.1 if applicable. Customer will need to verify prior claim history and acknowledge no further ADH claims count or incur service fees.
scenario.adjustments: Modified prior service events to include one initially treated as hardware defect under Section 3.1 rather than ADH; placed emphasis on ambiguity of prior two claims counting as ADH Service Events.
scenario.risk_analysis: This scenario is now closer to the coverage boundary because it is unclear if the prior two service events both fully counted against the two ADH claim limit; one event was repaired as a hardware defect rather than accidental damage. This ambiguity may provide grounds for interpretation as the current incident being covered as the second or third ADH claim, making the coverage decision less straightforward.

persona.defining quirks: Has a vast collection of antique books
persona.detailed job description: Retired university professor in the field of Fine Arts
persona.education: Professional degree beyond a bachelor's degree
persona.household type: Female householder, no spouse/partner present, living alone
persona.lifestyle: Quiet and solitary, with a focus on intellectual and creative pursuits
persona.mannerisms: Often lost in thought, tends to speak in a poetic manner
persona.personal time: Spends free time reading, painting or visiting art galleries

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

The current damage is caused by a drop resulting in screen and internal component failure, occurring shortly after the second ADH claim was serviced.

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello, I need to file a claim for my iPhone. It's the third accidental damage from handling issue I've had, and I think I qualify, but I'm a bit concerned about how my previous claims were classified. Can you help me with that?
messages.raw_text: Hello, I need to file a claim for my iPhone. It's the third accidental damage from handling issue I've had, and I think I qualify, but I'm a bit concerned about how my previous claims were classified. Can you help me with that?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Certainly, I can help you with your claim. To start, could you please provide the Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: Certainly, I can help you with your claim. To start, could you please provide the Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, my Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-8754210392, and the serial number is G6H9W3L1M4N7.
messages.raw_text: Sure, my Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-8754210392, and the serial number is G6H9W3L1M4N7.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe the issue or symptoms you're experiencing with your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe the issue or symptoms you're experiencing with your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, just recently, I dropped my phone, and now the screen is all cracked and the internal components aren't working well either. It’s frustrating because I thought everything was fine after the last repair, but I guess not. I just want to get this sorted out, you know?
messages.raw_text: Well, just recently, I dropped my phone, and now the screen is all cracked and the internal components aren't working well either. It’s frustrating because I thought everything was fine after the last repair, but I guess not. I just want to get this sorted out, you know?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I understand how frustrating that can be. To proceed, I need to ask a few diagnostic questions about your device. What model is your iPhone, and what is the current iOS version installed? Are you experiencing any error messages? Also, have you attached any peripherals recently?
messages.raw_text: I understand how frustrating that can be. To proceed, I need to ask a few diagnostic questions about your device. What model is your iPhone, and what is the current iOS version installed? Are you experiencing any error messages? Also, have you attached any peripherals recently?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: My device is an iPhone 14 Pro Max with 512 GB, and I can't really recall the iOS version at the moment. No error messages that I remember, but the screen is definitely cracked, and some inside parts seem damaged now. I haven't added any peripherals lately. I just want to get this handled without hassle.
messages.raw_text: My device is an iPhone 14 Pro Max with 512 GB, and I can't really recall the iOS version at the moment. No error messages that I remember, but the screen is definitely cracked, and some inside parts seem damaged now. I haven't added any peripherals lately. I just want to get this handled without hassle.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. Since your issue involves physical damage from a drop, could you please describe when, where, and how the accident occurred? A detailed description of the event will help us process your claim.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. Since your issue involves physical damage from a drop, could you please describe when, where, and how the accident occurred? A detailed description of the event will help us process your claim.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Oh, it was just late yesterday afternoon. I was outside in my garden, reaching for a book on a table, and the phone slipped from my hand. It tumbled onto the patio, and I heard the shatter. It all happened in a moment. I suppose I should have been more careful, but that's how it happened.
messages.raw_text: Oh, it was just late yesterday afternoon. I was outside in my garden, reaching for a book on a table, and the phone slipped from my hand. It tumbled onto the patio, and I heard the shatter. It all happened in a moment. I suppose I should have been more careful, but that's how it happened.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for sharing that information. Based on what you've described, it appears to be an accidental damage from handling (ADH) situation. Before we proceed, do you have any supporting photos or logs of the damage that you could upload? This can help with the processing of your claim.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for sharing that information. Based on what you've described, it appears to be an accidental damage from handling (ADH) situation. Before we proceed, do you have any supporting photos or logs of the damage that you could upload? This can help with the processing of your claim.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, I don't have any photos or logs. Honestly, I didn't think to take pictures after it happened. It's just a cracked screen and some internal stuff acting up now. I hope that won't be an issue. I just want to get this fixed quickly.
messages.raw_text: No, I don't have any photos or logs. Honestly, I didn't think to take pictures after it happened. It's just a cracked screen and some internal stuff acting up now. I hope that won't be an issue. I just want to get this fixed quickly.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: That's understandable. Your claim will be processed with the information you've provided. We will notify you as soon as it has been reviewed. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we conclude?
messages.raw_text: That's understandable. Your claim will be processed with the information you've provided. We will notify you as soon as it has been reviewed. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we conclude?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, I think that covers everything for now. I appreciate your help in getting this taken care of. Hopefully, it won't be too long before I can use my phone again. Thanks.
messages.raw_text: No, I think that covers everything for now. I appreciate your help in getting this taken care of. Hopefully, it won't be too long before I can use my phone again. Thanks.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome. Your claim will be processed, and you'll be notified once it's complete. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to contact us. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome. Your claim will be processed, and you'll be notified once it's complete. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to contact us. Have a great day! <END>
